records.ety: [F., from L. volumen a roll of writing, a book, volume, from volvere, volutum, to roll. See Voluble.]

defs: A roll; a scroll; a written document rolled up for keeping or for use, after the manner of the ancients.
quotes: The papyrus, and afterward the parchment, was joined together [by the ancients] to form one sheet, and then rolled upon a staff into a volume (volumen).

defs: Hence, a collection of printed sheets bound together, whether containing a single work, or a part of a work, or more than one work; a book; a tome; especially, that part of an extended work which is bound up together in one cover; as, a work in four volumes.
quotes: An odd volume of a set of books bears not the value of its proportion to the set.

defs: Anything of a rounded or swelling form resembling a roll; a turn; a convolution; a coil.
quotes: So glides some trodden serpent on the grass, And long behind wounded volume trails.
Undulating billows rolling their silver volumes.

defs: Dimensions; compass; space occupied, as measured by cubic units, that is, cubic inches, feet, yards, etc.; mass; bulk; as, the volume of an elephant's body; a volume of gas.
Amount, fullness, quantity, or caliber of voice or tone.
